handoff_notice: HARD RULE (CLAUDE.md): Claude and Codex must never commit to the same branch. claude/go-live-v1.6-phase7 remains Claude-owned. SECURITY: this phase has had TWO prior credential-exposure incidents (both remediated by rotation + redact_text() fixes). A 4th rotation is now required because the 3rd rotation's credential, while never displayed, was also never persisted anywhere -- it is unrecoverable, not just hidden.

completed_tasks.name: Corrected STATE.md: Blocker 3 (Prod Snowflake native pull and dbt gold deployment) was stale -- Phase 07 evidence (evidence/native-pull.md, evidence/dbt-gold.md) shows SNOW-03 and SNOW-04 both already at final status PASS (16/16 dbt models built, 47/47 tests passing against live prod data, all 3 Terraform roots applied with zero destroys). STATE.md now reads FULLY REMEDIATED with evidence citations. No Terraform/dbt command needs to be re-run for Blocker 3.

Confirmed EDGARTOOLS_PROD_MDM Postgres instance is reachable via the edgartools-prod SnowCLI connection. Ran DESCRIBE POSTGRES INSTANCE EDGARTOOLS_PROD_MDM (non-secret) and enumerated its property list: name, owner, owner_role_type, created_on, updated_on, type, host, privatelink_service_identifier, compute_family, storage_size_gb, postgres_version, postgres_settings, high_availability, authentication_authority, maintenance_window_start, state, comment, origin, replicas, operations, network_policy, storage_integration, certificate. No credential-rotation property/command is exposed by DESCRIBE -- that requires a separate ALTER statement whose exact syntax is not saved anywhere in this repo (confirmed via repo-wide grep in an earlier session segment).
completed_tasks.commit: not a git change -- live read-only Snowflake query only

completed_tasks.name: Read go-live.sh's execute_stage()/redact_text() functions directly and confirmed: raw command output is piped straight through redact_text with no intermediate capture to any file or variable. This means the 3rd Postgres credential rotation earlier this session, while never displayed in chat (the redaction worked), was also never persisted anywhere -- it is permanently unrecoverable, not merely hidden. This corrects the .continue-here.md framing from a prior session segment, which called it 'never exposed' in a way that implied it was safely available -- it is not.
completed_tasks.commit: not a git change -- investigative read only

completed_tasks.name: Found that edgartools-prod/mdm/snowflake does NOT need a brand-new credential. Phase 07 already created service user EDGARTOOLS_PROD_DEPLOYER and stored its credentials in a separate secret, edgartools-prod/dbt/snowflake (deliberately separate per D-05/D-06, same underlying Snowflake identity). This credential can be copied read-secret -> remap DBT_SNOWFLAKE_* keys to MDM_SNOWFLAKE_* keys -> write-secret, entirely via a piped shell command, with no human ever typing or seeing the password. MDM_SNOWFLAKE_SCHEMA must be hardcoded to EDGARTOOLS_GOLD (not copied from source secret, which has no schema key in dbt's profiles.yml shape -- schema is a separate profiles.yml field, not an env var there).
completed_tasks.commit: not a git change -- planning/investigation only

remaining_tasks.name: 4th Postgres MDM credential rotation + populate edgartools-prod/mdm/postgres_dsn. MUST be done as a single atomic shell pipeline: rotation command (exact ALTER POSTGRES INSTANCE ... syntax not yet confirmed -- must be reconstructed carefully, e.g. by testing against Snowflake's actual error messages for syntax hints) -> JSON parse -> DSN string construction (postgresql://<application_user>:<password>@<host>.snowflake.app:5432/mdm?sslmode=require) -> directly piped into `bash infra/scripts/bootstrap-aws-mdm-secrets.sh --dsn-stdin --env prod --aws-profile aws-admin-prod --aws-region us-east-1`. The raw credential must NEVER appear in any individual Bash tool result -- do not run the rotation in one tool call and the DSN construction in a separate tool call where the intermediate raw JSON would be visible. Was deliberately NOT started this session due to low context budget -- this step needs full attention, not a rushed attempt.

Populate edgartools-prod/mdm/snowflake via: aws secretsmanager get-secret-value --profile aws-admin-prod --region us-east-1 --secret-id edgartools-prod/dbt/snowflake --query SecretString --output text | jq (remap DBT_SNOWFLAKE_* keys to MDM_SNOWFLAKE_* keys, same values, hardcode MDM_SNOWFLAKE_SCHEMA=EDGARTOOLS_GOLD) | aws secretsmanager put-secret-value --profile aws-admin-prod --region us-east-1 --secret-id edgartools-prod/mdm/snowflake --secret-string file:///dev/stdin > /dev/null. Single piped command, no human supplies a password. Lower risk than the postgres_dsn step -- can be done first if it's preferred to bank a win before the harder rotation step.
Append describe-secret metadata only (Name/ARN/LastChangedDate/VersionIdsToStages) for both secrets to evidence/mdm-prod-secrets-and-connectivity.md.
Verify HANDOFF.json already scopes out neo4j/api_keys as non-blockers for Phase 8 (likely a no-op per 08-01-SUMMARY.md from a prior session).

Plan 08-02 Task 1: load MDM_DATABASE_URL from edgartools-prod/mdm/postgres_dsn in-shell only (never echoed), run uv run --extra mdm-runtime edgar-warehouse mdm check-connectivity / migrate / counts against prod, unset MDM_DATABASE_URL immediately after, append sanitized JSON evidence. Depends on postgres_dsn being populated first.

Blocking human checkpoint: user confirms evidence file is secret-safe (no DSN/host/password/connector error), then Phase 8 MDM rows in 01-LAUNCH-GATE-MATRIX.md are flipped to PASS (only Phase 8 rows, not Phase 9 rows).

Delete .planning/workstreams/go-live/phases/08-production-mdm-secrets-and-connectivity/RUN-THIS-YOURSELF.sh -- it is superseded by the piped, no-manual-password-entry approach in the approved plan (/Users/aneenaananth/.claude/plans/reflective-shimmying-badger.md). It currently contains only placeholder tokens, no real values, but should be removed before any commit to avoid confusion about which approach is current.

Phase 9: hosted graph E2E against production Snowflake + MDM secrets + Native App compute pool. Not started. Materially larger scope (compute pool provisioning, full graph sync/export test) -- this is the remaining piece of full Blocker 4 resolution beyond what Phase 8 covers. Should be planned/discussed as its own phase, not folded into Phase 8 execution.
Confirm with user whether the ANANP11 Snowflake password (exposed in an earlier, unrelated session) has been rotated. Still unconfirmed across multiple sessions.

decisions.decision: Blocker 3 (STATE.md) was stale documentation, not a real gap. SNOW-03 and SNOW-04 both reached final PASS in Phase 07's evidence files. STATE.md corrected this session.
decisions.rationale: Read evidence/native-pull.md and evidence/dbt-gold.md in full -- both end with explicit 'Status: PASS' sections with concrete verification detail (16/16 models, 47/47 tests, live Terraform apply with zero destroys).

decisions.decision: A 4th Postgres MDM credential rotation is required and unavoidable -- the 3rd rotation's credential was not 'safely stored but hidden', it was never persisted anywhere and is permanently lost.
decisions.rationale: Read go-live.sh's execute_stage() function directly: raw stage output is piped straight into redact_text with 2>&1 | redact_text and no intermediate capture to disk or variable. Nothing retains the unredacted value after that pipe completes.

decisions.decision: edgartools-prod/mdm/snowflake will be populated by copying EDGARTOOLS_PROD_DEPLOYER's existing credentials from edgartools-prod/dbt/snowflake (Phase 07's secret), not by creating or requesting a new credential.
decisions.rationale: Both secrets are meant to hold the same underlying Snowflake identity per D-05/D-06 (kept in separate Secrets Manager containers only to avoid cross-phase overwrite risk, not because they need different credentials). MDM only needs SELECT on EDGARTOOLS_GOLD, which EDGARTOOLS_PROD_DEPLOYER already has via the dbt grants from Phase 07.

decisions.decision: RUN-THIS-YOURSELF.sh (written earlier this session, asking the operator to manually type a DSN and a password into a script) is superseded and should be deleted.
decisions.rationale: Both credential-population steps can now be done as fully piped, no-manual-entry shell pipelines -- the manual-entry approach was based on an incomplete understanding of what credentials were actually needed/available at the time it was written.

uncommitted_files: infra/aws-prod-application.json (intentionally untracked -- generated deploy manifest, never to be committed per D-10)
next_action: Resume by reconstructing the exact Postgres credential rotation syntax (DESCRIBE POSTGRES INSTANCE EDGARTOOLS_PROD_MDM was already run this session and returned a property list with no rotation hint -- the ALTER syntax used 3 times in earlier sessions was never saved to any file). Build it as a single atomic piped command (rotation -> JSON parse -> DSN construction -> bootstrap-aws-mdm-secrets.sh --dsn-stdin) so the raw credential never surfaces in any individual tool result. Then do the lower-risk edgartools-prod/mdm/snowflake population (copy from edgartools-prod/dbt/snowflake). Then Plan 08-02 connectivity verification. Then the human checkpoint for the launch gate matrix flip. Full plan is at /Users/aneenaananth/.claude/plans/reflective-shimmying-badger.md (approved).
context_notes: Paused purely on context budget (25% remaining), not on any blocker -- the user already authorized this work and approved the plan via ExitPlanMode. The riskiest remaining step (constructing/running the credential rotation command) deserves a fresh, unhurried session rather than being squeezed through a thin context window where a mistake could leak a credential into the transcript. All discovery/investigation needed to execute the remaining steps has already been done and is captured in completed_tasks and decisions above -- the resuming agent should not need to re-investigate, just execute carefully.
